interpretation: A corresponding change in the person's character, disposition, and nature. Modifications in nature due to experience and vicissitudes lead to alterations in handwriting.
activity
nervous, quick and energetic dispositions
mental alertness and physical restlessness
natural workers
not happy unless their brains or hands are busy
whatever they do is done with energy and enthusiasm
tastes turn to the practical affairs of life
natural aptitude for work requiring manual skill
apt to be emphatic, precise, positive and aggressive
generally self-reliant and industrious
fond of the pleasures, the beauties, the comforts, the luxuries of the world
work for work's sake does not appeal to them
not naturally industrious
lovers of peace, rest and enjoyment
do not like to be disturbed
natures are more or less passive
benevolent and kind-hearted
have loving dispositions rather than affectionate or ardent
natural desire is to go through life as easily and as comfortably as possible
careful to avoid all unnecessary burdens
personality in which the emotions, the feelings, the sympathies, constitute the natural governing element
shows susceptibility to sentiment
natural tendency of a writer is to be guided by his impulses

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: governed very largely by sympathies and feelings

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: extremely affectionate

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: kind and sympathetic

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: generous and self-sacrificing

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: inclined to be impulsive, animated and demonstrative

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: capable of intense love and passion

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: easily pleased

variation: Forward Hand (very marked slope to the right)
interpretation: readily stirred to enthusiasm
in a general sense amenable to the common influences of life
not so readily susceptible to the influences of their environment or their emotions

natures are not necessarily calmer or more evenly balanced, but they endeavor to conduct themselves more deliberately; judgment and reason and self-interest enter into their motives and into their actions; not apt to become very enthusiastic, nor are they inclined to be ardent in their attachments; whatever they do is tempered with self-restraint and deliberation; do not like to be hurried; generally slow, and do not arrive at decisions very readily; simplicity of taste, deliberateness of thought and speech, and outward calmness (not always naturally calm, arid often subject themselves to repression of feeling and emotion); apt to talk slowly and act with much deliberation and precision; do not like to do anything hastily or on the spur of the moment; everything must be given due consideration and attention; affections may be deep, but are not often intense or demonstrative; sincere, constant, and loyal; usually take a practical, common-sense view of life, and are not readily disturbed in their purposes and beliefs

deliberate, dignified person; a lover of the beautiful; a man of careful habits and simple tastes; a person of self-control and balance; calmness, a finish, a clearness (about his poetry); characteristic due to his love of order and detail; one of the qualities indicated by a vertical handwriting
deliberate, dignified person; a lover of the beautiful; a man of careful habits and simple tastes; a person of self-control and balance; calmness, a finish, a clearness (about his poetry)
involved, impetuous and rugged style
involved, impetuous and rugged style

reason and self-interest are the governing motives; sentiment or susceptibility to sentiment is not lacking but is rarely permitted to influence their actions or their decisions, if it is likely to interfere with their comfort or plans; natural reasoners and schemers; personal interests come first in the decision of all questions; sympathies are held in check; do not often have very deep or lasting affections; think more of themselves than they do of anybody else; nearly always self-conscious; seldom does anything impulsively or without first thinking of the consequences; has a dread of appearing in a false light to others, or of creating an impression unfavorable to himself; inclined to be artful and insincere; persons of ability, originality and much self-reliance (this latter characteristic is often very strongly marked); usually unorthodox in their beliefs; seldom enthusiastic or deeply interested in the general affairs of the world; not the kind of persons who engage in charitable work or philanthropic enterprises; not bound to principles of morality and good behavior because of any religious regard for such principles (if good and moral it is simply because they choose to be so as a matter of self-interest); natural aptitude for executive and administrative work; make good leaders, but poor followers; have but little regard for authority; generally quiet and reserved, and do not easily become excited nor enthusiastic; rarely demonstrative in their affections

intellectuality, good reasoning powers, culture, and originality of ideas; capable of concentration, both mental and physical; natural brain workers and can endure a large amount of fatigue; persons of individuality and force of character; mental powers are above the average and their general abilities and capacities are correspondingly pronounced; naturally versatile, well-informed, acquisitive of knowledge, and good thinkers and reasoners; apt to be self-contained and patient, with a natural reticence concerning their personal affairs; not apt to make a display of feeling in public; have the gift of keeping silence, and biding their time; reserve, quietness and dignity are often associated; self-sufficient; may enjoy society and the companionship of their fellows, but they seldom crave it or find it essential to their well-being; usually best contented when alone with their own thoughts and work

methodical ways; neat, self-reliant and thorough; executive and business qualities, in which perspicacity, judgment and sometimes shrewdness play important parts; seldom do anything hastily or ill-advisedly, but are governed always by well trained faculties

methodical ways; neat, self-reliant and thorough; executive and business qualities, in which perspicacity, judgment and sometimes shrewdness play important parts; seldom do anything hastily or ill-advisedly, but are governed always by well trained faculties

cunning and suspicion; cunning is always that of a diplomat; finesse and diplomacy; wary and alert, difficult to convict, clever at plausible arguments and evasions, and always careful to provide a loophole for escape from any dilemma

cunning and suspicion; cunning is always that of a diplomat; finesse and diplomacy; wary and alert, difficult to convict, clever at plausible arguments and evasions, and always careful to provide a loophole for escape from any dilemma

intellectuality, literary and business ability, educational culture, reservedness, diplomacy, self-containment, patience, industry, quietness, thoughtfulness, mental energy, perspicacity, and judgment

character devoted more to the practical, material affairs of life, than to the intellectual or the spiritual; outspoken, frank and talkative, and possess but little tact; express their feelings without reserve; natures are close to the surface and are easily seen, for they make little or no effort to conceal their emotions or opinions; apt to be critical and to find fault (although in the great majority of instances, their bark is worse than their bite); quick to take others to task and to cast aspersions upon the conduct of their friends and acquaintances; seldom deeply in earnest, and their comments generally should be taken with due allowance for their impulsiveness and lack of restraint; but slight power of concentration; their thoughts are diffuse and fly readily from one subject to another; find it difficult to remain long at one task; naturally restless and unsettled, and hard to satisfy
Naturally generous and sympathetic.

Fond of that which is small or dainty; observes details and particularities very quickly and often gives much concern over them; very entertaining talkers (talk about themselves or personal affairs/experiences); insists upon having things their own way; apt to be extremely selfish and thoughtless.

variation: Large with flourishes and deliberate shading
interpretation: Egotistical and pompous, and much given to boasting.
Denotes ardor, zeal and enthusiasm; readily animated or excited; always vivacious and spirited.
Assurance, aggressiveness and a general lack of delicacy (especially when writing is above average in size).

Roughness and boldness of manner; overbearing, a braggart, and in all probability a bully (if corroborated); possessed of strong passions and animal appetites; usually muscular, fond of physical exercise, with desires confined largely to the gross and the material; pugnacious disposition; successes in life due solely to assurance and aggressiveness; unacquainted with modesty in asking or demanding favors; forges ahead where others would hesitate.
Domineering and exacting disposition (traits shown in man's writing are modified but possessed in a greater or less degree, ready to manifest).

Intensely selfish and conceited; little regard for the feelings of others; inclined to be deceitful and revengeful; will not hesitate to resort to trickery; always ready to boast of exploits and accomplishments; masterful, self-reliant, and frequently possess much ability.

variation: Conscious shading (done for effect, to add to penmanship effect)
interpretation: Affectation, egotism and a commonplace personality; nearly always an indication of small education, and lack of culture and ability.

variation: Unconscious shading (on t-crosses and terminal strokes)
interpretation: Insistent nature (more pronounced shading = more insistent and emphatic); natural executive ability, coupled with a fondness for and an appreciation of physical pleasures; fond of luxury and good living; distinctive personality, with well-defined tastes; chief signification: insistence and determination.

variation: Fine (delicate strokes, marked absence of shading or pressure)
interpretation: A person of quiet, modest tastes, and with a disposition that tends toward complaisance and simplicity.

variation: Very fine (all letters formed of very fine strokes)
interpretation: A person of refined and delicate sensibilities, temperate in habit, quiet and unobtrusive, and with little if any desire for voluptuous pleasures or luxuries of life.
Commonplace personality.

Lacks individuality; no distinctive character; limited ideas and capacities; little originality; a copyist, an imitator, bound by rules and conventionalities; no ideas of his own; nothing mentally above the dead level of the commonplace; merely one of thousands; accomplishments limited and never brilliant.

variation: Fancy writing (flourishes, graceful curves, ornamented capitals, fanciful strokes, artistic shading)
interpretation: Much impressed with his own importance; egotistical and conceited; the more wonderful and elaborate, the more insignificant the person (despite an excellent opinion of self); often affected in manner; endeavors to have it believed that he is really of importance; inclined to be headstrong and opinionated; lays much stress on trifling matters of form and style.

variation: plain or inartistic (schoolboy hand), often awkward, simple, legible, unaffected, distinctive
interpretation: modesty, frankness, truthfulness, sincerity, unassuming, honest, trustworthy, guided by motives of integrity, no patience with deceit, rises superior to affectation, does duty, careful to do what is right regardless of others' opinions

variation: neat and uniform, letters carefully and distinctly formed
interpretation: corresponding neatness, precision, carefulness, tactful, methodical, painstaking, generally well-balanced, systematic, likes to do things according to system, does things well and thoroughly, good business instincts, thrifty, prudent, economical

variation: lack of precision in formation or general unevenness
interpretation: greater or less degree of carelessness, not naturally methodical or systematic, often careless about little things, inclined to be indifferent as to engagements or business affairs

variation: straggling and untidy penmanship with marked lack of uniformity
interpretation: weak, vacillating character; absence of judgment, method or discretion; little self-control; apt to be flighty and irresponsible; lacks poise and mental balance

variation: many letters only partially formed or consist of mere unformed strokes, general lack of distinctness, must be read quickly and in jumps
interpretation: quickness, hastiness, impatience, vivacity, animation, good talker, very entertaining, possesses much ability, accomplished, originality, puts stamp of individuality, little patience with rules or humdrum work, apt to be insincere, does not always intend statements to be accepted literally, dissimulation, artfulness, conscientiousness not a strong point, seldom bothers with details, does things in a hurry, does not care if according to rule

variation: each letter separately formed but difficult to tell apart as they are all made so nearly alike (not slurred)
interpretation: quickness, animation, vivacity, much hastiness, intolerance of details, inherent inability to do things according to plan or order, impulsive, insists on doing things agreeably to own notions, will not follow recipes/rules/dictation, lacks ability or aptitude for practical work, lacks necessary patience to follow directions or remain steadily at one task

variation: each letter separately formed but difficult to tell apart as they are all made so nearly alike (not slurred)
interpretation: does not denote dissimulation, finesse, diplomacy or lack of frankness

variation: words and lines crowded well together, writing appears crowded on the page, fills every available space, squeezes words in corners, fills space at head of letter rather than use another sheet
interpretation: saving disposition, inclined to worry, particular about little things, close, takes narrow views of life, naturally secretive, cramped nature, often warped, instinctive desire to save paper, economy, closeness, parsimony

variation: crowded along the right-hand margin so words look squeezed
interpretation: suspicious natures

variation: diffuse, spreads over the page, large amount of space with comparatively little writing, ample space between words and lines, often associated with large hands
interpretation: naturally generous or extravagant, finds it difficult to save, intolerant of restraint, wishes to be left free to do what they please and spend as much as they please, neither economical nor thrifty (unless circumstances necessitate), not methodical nor systematic, dislikes drudgery, often merely superficial

variation: Letters spread apart within words (loose writing/diffuse words)
interpretation: Slovenliness, mental weakness, characteristic of lunatics, lack of strong mentality (may be temporary lack of mental vigor due to illness), ideas are diffuse and not easily concentrated

variation: Carefully and uniformly spaced (letters, words, lines, no letter collisions between lines)
interpretation: Good judgment, well-ordered mind, dependable in matters calling for circumspection and prudence, rarely acts hastily in business, not readily flustered or convinced against better judgment, good manager, careful, conservative, business instincts

variation: Letters of one line collide/extend into the line below
interpretation: More or less carelessness (in indifference to engagements, duties, minor proprieties), tendency to flightiness or lack of poise, not naturally methodical or systematic, judgment in important affairs not often reliable, not always dependable, changes mind quickly, unstable decisions, plans liable to sudden alteration, little taste for routine business, rash and imprudent in financial affairs, prefers making money by speculation

variation: Lines not evenly or regularly spaced (but not colliding)
interpretation: Not naturally gifted with methodical or business instincts, tendency to carelessness and lack of precision

variation: Straight, even lines (across the page without sloping up or down, and even throughout with all words/letters on the line)
interpretation: Perseverance, will power, firmness of character

Ambitious, hopeful, not easily discouraged, good nature (especially if writing is rounded), cheerful view of life, optimistic, ready to try again, determination to succeed, never satisfied with accomplishments, constantly pushing ahead, ideals and aspirations, looks forward to the future

variation: Very marked upward slope (exaggerated ascending lines)
interpretation: Exaggerated ambition, more ambitious than practical, visionary, extravagant aspirations

variation: Descending lines (droops toward lower right-hand corner)
interpretation: Lack of initiative, lack of active ambition, lack of vigor or steadfastness of purpose, wanting in self-reliance or hopefulness, pessimistic, easily discouraged, easily put out of humor, habit of worrying and fretting, assumes the worst will happen, subject to morbid prejudices, often suspicious, takes offense quickly, misconstrues others' words/actions, uncharitable and unreasonable views
depression; ill-health; diminution of mental vigor
artistic tastes (fond of neat and harmonious); self-conscious; studies appearances; inclined to be fussy over little things; bothers with trifles

variation: connected (letters well joined within a word, pen not lifted)
interpretation: practical turn of mind; instinctively careful and prudent; does not make up mind hastily on matters of importance; natural reasoner; takes very little for granted; inclined to ask questions (must know the why and wherefore); argumentative; tendency to question statements and opinions of others; skeptical of whatever he does not at once understand; accepts very little on faith; opinionated; holds very decided views; facts must be proved according to principles of logic and reason; disputes or declines to accept unproven facts; often seen in business men and lawyers

variation: connected words (two or three words strung together without lifting pen)
interpretation: accentuates qualities of connected letters; very decided convictions; great argumentativeness; deep prejudices; hard to convince; very tenacious of his opinions; insists upon full and definite understandings in all business matters; actions governed always by judgment and reason; never permits intuitions or feelings to influence him in practical affairs of life; rarely jumps at conclusions; views on any subject fortified by reason, facts and argument; does nothing without cause; subscribes to no tenet or belief without assuring himself it accords with his judgment; keen, shrewd, and exacting in business; difficult to overreach him

variation: disconnected (failure to write each word with a continuous stroke; letters broken within words)
interpretation: found much more frequently in women than men; essentially a feminine characteristic; intuition; jumps at conclusions; makes up mind quickly; arrives at decisions instinctively; judgment dictated by inner consciousness rather than deliberate reasoning; quick of comprehension; observant; critical; little escapes their notice; good judges of character; likes and dislikes formed on the spur of the moment; guided largely by impressions; first impressions are most trustworthy and correct; better to be guided by impressions than arguments or reason; nimble-witted; excellent insight; not easy to deceive; quick to detect real motives; grasp problems readily; jump at once from premises to conclusion; apt to be superficial in knowledge and accomplishments; beliefs and opinions based on faith rather than logic; believes because they feel it is right (cannot articulate why); not fond of argument; resents criticism or analysis of opinions; satisfied with opinions/decisions; does not fortify opinions with arguments for skeptics; naturally religious; ready to accept as true whatever appeals to sentiment or ideas; leanings nearly always toward the spiritual, occult, or mysterious rather than the materialistic

variation: disconnected capital (capital letter not connected to the rest of the word)
interpretation: slight significance; cannot properly be classed under Connected Letters; partakes of significations of Disconnected Letters to a limited extent

accentuated qualities of general disconnected letters; usually idealists; intuitions are the dominant controlling force of their lives; guided almost wholly by imaginations, impressions, subconsciousness; acts and words largely inspirational; students of the occult often write in this manner; characteristic of persons gifted with psychic
Characteristic of artists, poets, musicians, or those whose work is essentially inspirational or emotional; often found in men of genius in art, poetry, music, oratory.

variation: Increasing size towards the end of a word (last letter larger than first)
interpretation: Conscientiousness; naturally honest, straightforward, over-conscientious; does more than duty; fussy over trivial matters of propriety or honesty; exaggerates importance of duty; sets high standards; unreasonably exacting; values consistency and allegiance to principles; consistent in views and conduct; decisions determined by principle.

variation: Decreasing size towards the end of a word (tapering off)
interpretation: Lack of frankness; qualities opposite to increasing size writers; conscientiousness is not obtrusive or interfering; high ideals but not necessarily bound by them; expediency is more important than consistency or principle; concedes circumstances alter cases; diplomatic, tactful, shrewd; often selfish; not always dependable; capricious; difficult to understand; rarely expresses themselves unreservedly; reluctance to expose true feelings; lacking in directness and candor.

variation: Very marked decreasing size (final letters dwindle into mere horizontal strokes)
interpretation: Correspondingly pronounced characteristics of decreasing size writers (e.g., pronounced lack of frankness, diplomacy, selfishness).

variation: Variable sizes within a word (some small, some large, no uniformity)
interpretation: Versatility; natural ability to do many things well; apt to lack poise; rather changeable; subject to whims and fancies.

variation: Plain (simply made, modest, simple, relatively small, no ornamentation, enlarged small letters, imitations of printed letters)
interpretation: Taste, refinement, modesty, simplicity; quiet artistic temperaments; characteristic of men of genius, artistic refinement and ability; characteristic of literary men, deep thinkers, and philosophers; inherent ability; mental culture; rarely egotistical or conceited; modest about accomplishments; simple and refined tastes; no ostentation of manner, no affectation of deportment or speech, no assumption of qualities; quiet dignity or ingenuous frankness of true merit and worth.

variation: Conspicuously large or ornamental (large and showy)
interpretation: Essentially egotistical; egotism, conceit, affectation, love of display, pride; writer is insignificant (except in own estimation); places great store by own importance; gives undue weight to trivial personal matters; mediocre talents and accomplishments; not a person of intellectual depth; naturally vain and egotistical; conceited and boastful in talk or quietly self-appreciative and fond of approbation; exhibits self-importance.

variation: Simply large (without ornamentation, scrolls, or unnecessary flourishes)
interpretation: Egotism and pride (to a certain extent); boldness; aggressiveness; self-reliance; masterfulness.

variation: large and ornamental (inconsistent with quiet, modest, unassuming hand)
interpretation: eccentricity or originality

variation: initial stroke cuts across the body of the letter (Cross-cut Capital)
interpretation: egotism, a good opinion of oneself (regarding accomplishments or personality), self-appreciative, much self-reliance, very intolerant of adverse criticism
ambitious for the success or welfare of those dear to them, pride in others (e.g., a mother for a child, a wife for a husband), egotism once removed

variation: last stroke taller than the first two
interpretation: unbalanced mind, nervous, eccentric, unreasonable disposition, exacting in demands upon others, thoughtless of others' comfort or capabilities, a trying and wearing disposition

reserve, lack of frankness, desire as well as ability to keep one's own counsel, not in the habit of taking others into confidence, knows how to keep a secret, naturally tactful, cautious and discreet
secretiveness, very reserved, uncommunicative disposition, rarely talks about oneself or affairs, difficult to draw out on any subject, careful not to commit oneself

variation: failure to close at the top (open)
interpretation: frank, outspoken, does not hesitate to express opinions or feelings, generally ready to talk, difficult to keep a secret, tendency to take others into personal confidence regarding family matters or domestic affairs, seldom reserved, inclined to be communicative, remarks often ill-timed or indiscreet, little tact

trait_name: letter b, h, k, l, or t
variation: initial stroke cuts across the body of the letter (Cross-Cut Letter)
interpretation: egotistical, self-appreciative, proud, self-reliant

trait_name: letter b, f, h, k, or l
variation: initial hook (instead of a loop in the upper part)
interpretation: talkativeness, fond of conversation, always has much to say, usually a rapid and animated talker

variation: unlooped upper formation (mere stroke instead of a well-defined loop)
interpretation: conciseness and practical ideas and abilities, originality or distinctive individuality, not usually commonplace, decisiveness, firm convictions, does not hesitate to carry them out
without any special significance

variation: upper portion disproportionately high (above standard height relative to other letters, possibly equal to or overtopping t, A, or h)
interpretation: pride and self-respect (not to be confused with conceit and vanity), proud of their family or ancestry

pride of person or of family; an inherent dignity and respect for one's self. These persons never condescend to anything that will compromise or demean their standard of personal quality. They have a high sense of honor, which at times is almost fanatical in its strictness.

not necessarily a lack of self-respect, but the writers of such a d have not that same regard for their personal dignity as is manifested in the high d. They may possess much personal pride, but they are willing, in a pinch, to waive it in favor of some personal gratification or profit. It does not form an essential part of their nature. It is put on and taken off to suit the conditions in which the writers find themselves. They have not that same high sense of honor that distinguishes the writers of the tall d. Their consciences are more elastic and their standards are not so clearly defined. They allow themselves more scope for action without compromising their dignity or their morals.

variation: curved (conventional method, terminating in a graceful curve or hook)
interpretation: literary persons. Either they have literary tastes, or are writers or critics. It is a sign of scholarly attainments, literary discrimination and a nicety of taste in the cultivated arts. They are usually persons of ability and are more or less accomplished, particularly in the domain of literature.

variation: decidedly toward the right, letter leaning forward
interpretation: coquettish nature, especially when associated with large or diffuse writing. The writer of such a d is inclined to be gay, fun-loving and fanciful with a tendency to be flirtatious.

analytical as well as a critical mind. The writers of this style of d have a tendency to analyze their thoughts and feelings, as well as the motives and actions of those about them. They are apt to be sensitive and easily prejudiced. Their nature is largely introspective; their inclination is to draw within themselves. They do not care for the indiscriminate society of their fellow-beings. They would rather be isolated than be obliged to associate with those who are not congenial.

variation: looped (return stroke widely separated from up-stroke)
interpretation: sensitiveness. The pride of the writer is easily wounded, and his feelings are readily hurt. He takes offense quickly, and often at mere trifles. It takes but little to make him feel that he has been wronged or insulted.

extreme morbidness on the subject of one's pride and rights. It is a peculiarity often seen in the writing of lunatics, and invariably denotes unreasonable sensitiveness and a generally morbid condition of mind.

variation: descends below the line of writing
interpretation: strongly opinionated. This feature is always an indication of decided views, strong convictions, and usually narrow prejudices. Obstinacy is nearly always present in the writer's make-up, and wilfulness is usually indicated also. Such a writer clings to his or her opinions through thick and thin, and the more they are opposed the more determined becomes the writer to hold on to them.
a person of conventional ideas and tastes, with a natural inclination to whatever appeals to the fancy or the emotions.

variation: prominent by reason of its size or some unusualness of its formation
interpretation: cleverness, a lively imagination and a quick and vivid fancy. The person who makes his f's, g's, j's, y's and z's with long and well rounded loops is fond of poetry, music or art—whatever appeals to the imagination—and is more or less dominated by his impressions and fancies, as opposed to the one who is guided by strict reason and principles of business and order.

variation: so long that it extends into the line below
interpretation: writer is inclined to be romantic and notional and to hold rather unconventional ideas on many subjects. This peculiarity... is found more frequently in the writing of women than of men. When it occurs it may be taken for granted that the writer is fond of novelty and originality. Whatever is odd appeals to her at once. She is a lover of fads, especially such as are supposed to be fashionable, for she is always abreast of the times in styles and notions, and quickly adopts whatever 'society' endorses as the proper caper, and drops it as soon as it is no longer regarded as good form. She is apt to be extravagant in her mode of living, as she is not really content unless in style in matters of dress and household appointments. She is not usually much of a business woman, being too venturesome and speculative. The disposition of such writers is usually restless and calls for frequent change and variety.

variation: made after the manner of the loop in the letter e
interpretation: hastiness; a desire on the part of the writer to get through with his tasks as rapidly as possible, in order to have them off his hands.

conservatism of ideas, practical and methodical traits; a good deal of firmness, will power and self-reliance. Fancy and imagination, while often present in abundance, are held in check; they are not allowed to carry the writer beyond the bounds of reasonableness or utility. Such writers are utilitarian in their tastes and ideas rather than idealistic or fanciful.

much firmness and determination. Such a writer is inclined to be obstinate and hard to convince against his wishes. The more firmly and the more heavily the stroke is made, the more clearly are these traits evidenced.
a person of conventional ideas and tastes, with a natural inclination to whatever appeals to the fancy or the emotions.

variation: prominent by reason of its size or some unusualness of its formation

variation: so long that it extends into the line below
interpretation: writer is inclined to be romantic and notional and to hold rather unconventional ideas on many subjects. This peculiarity... is found more frequently in the writing of women than of men. When it occurs it may be taken for granted that the writer is fond of novelty and originality. Whatever is odd appeals to her at once. She is a lover of fads, especially such as are supposed to be fashionable, for she is always abreast of the times in styles and notions, and quickly adopts whatever 'society' endorses as the proper caper, and drops it as soon as it is no longer regarded as good form. She is apt to be extravagant in her mode of living, as she is not really content unless in style in matters of dress and household appointments. She is not usually much of a business woman, being too venturesome and speculative. The disposition of such writers is usually restless and calls for frequent change and variety.

variation: made after the manner of the loop in the letter e
interpretation: hastiness; a desire on the part of the writer to get through with his tasks as rapidly as possible, in order to have them off his hands.

conservatism of ideas, practical and methodical traits; a good deal of firmness, will power and self-reliance. Fancy and imagination, while often present in abundance, are held in check; they are not allowed to carry the writer beyond the bounds of reasonableness or utility. Such writers are utilitarian in their tastes and ideas rather than idealistic or fanciful.

much firmness and determination. Such a writer is inclined to be obstinate and hard to convince against his wishes. The more firmly and the more heavily the stroke is made, the more clearly are these traits evidenced.
a person of conventional ideas and tastes, with a natural inclination to whatever appeals to the fancy or the emotions.

variation: prominent by reason of its size or some unusualness of its formation
interpretation: cleverness, a lively imagination and a quick and vivid fancy. The person who makes his f's, g's, j's, y's and z's with long and well rounded loops is fond of poetry, music or art—whatever appeals to the imagination—and is more or less dominated by his impressions and fancies, as opposed to the one who is guided by strict reason and principles of business and order.

variation: so long that it extends into the line below
interpretation: writer is inclined to be romantic and notional and to hold rather unconventional ideas on many subjects. This peculiarity... is found more frequently in the writing of women than of men. When it occurs it may be taken for granted that the writer is fond of novelty and originality. Whatever is odd appeals to her at once. She is a lover of fads, especially such as are supposed to be fashionable, for she is always abreast of the times in styles and notions, and quickly adopts whatever 'society' endorses as the proper caper, and drops it as soon as it is no longer regarded as good form. She is apt to be extravagant in her mode of living, as she is not really content unless in style in matters of dress and household appointments. She is not usually much of a business woman, being too venturesome and speculative. The disposition of such writers is usually restless and calls for frequent change and variety.

variation: made after the manner of the loop in the letter e
interpretation: hastiness; a desire on the part of the writer to get through with his tasks as rapidly as possible, in order to have them off his hands.

conservatism of ideas, practical and methodical traits; a good deal of firmness, will power and self-reliance. Fancy and imagination, while often present in abundance, are held in check; they are not allowed to carry the writer beyond the bounds of reasonableness or utility. Such writers are utilitarian in their tastes and ideas rather than idealistic or fanciful.

much firmness and determination. Such a writer is inclined to be obstinate and hard to convince against his wishes. The more firmly and the more heavily the stroke is made, the more clearly are these traits evidenced.

variation: placed to the right of the letter (not unusually far)
interpretation: little or no significance unless the dot is placed unusually far to the right.

variation: placed far to the right and well above the letter
interpretation: quickness, vivacity, animation, impulsiveness and impatience. As a rule, however, this sign simply confirms the characteristics shown in other features of the handwriting.
deliberation and slowness, with a tendency toward procrastination.

variation: placed to the left side of the letter
interpretation: finds it hard to make up his mind or to carry out his purposes. He lacks steadfastness and continuity of mental effort. His mind and his actions do not always work in harmony. He allows his impulse often to govern him, not because he is naturally impulsive, but because he cannot get his mind to determine for him. This accentuates deliberation and slowness, and a tendency toward procrastination.

physical agility; a love of exercise; of athletics; of outdoor sports. Such persons are usually good dancers and of good muscular development. Sports and recreation of all kinds calling for physical exercise appeal to them.
its significance, in addition to the personal characteristics mentioned for a long p-lower-part, is the same as that ascribed to the loop formations of f, g, j, etc., Section 44.

variation: tightly closed in the lower portion
interpretation: not a person of candor; a disposition to dodge the truth on occasion; whose assertions must be accepted with allowance and who can rarely be depended upon to be perfectly frank and straightforward in his business dealings.

a careful and painstaking person. It signifies good will power and constancy of purpose. Such a person is naturally methodical, conscientious and more or less deliberate. He likes to do things right and according to rule, and it is not likely to relinquish a task until it is properly completed.

firmness, and much will power and determination. a person of great determination, much aggressiveness, assurance and self-reliance. He insists upon having his own way, and is possessed of that force of character that generally accords him that right. He is positive in his views and determined in his purposes. a sign of force and energy, strong will, physical courage, daring and persistence of effort.

variation: heavily and largely crossed with decided firmness
interpretation: adds emphasis to the qualities of firmness, much will power and determination, great determination, aggressiveness, assurance, self-reliance, positivity, force, energy, strong will, physical courage, daring and persistence of effort. Such a writer goes ahead boldly and with much assertiveness. He permits nothing to daunt him, and is often defiant in his manner.

variation: weakly crossed (insignificant in size and strength or absent)
interpretation: a lack of strong will power. Persons are usually without much force of character. They lack firmness and decision. They are not likely to be steadfast in their aims or purposes, unless their pathway is smooth and easy. They are easily dissuaded, vacillating, irresolute and are wanting in continuity of energy. They have not the self-reliance, persistence, and determination of other writers. This feature is rarely found in the writing of persons who hold responsible positions in life or persons of marked ability, belonging essentially to those of mediocre and commonplace capabilities, those who hold the minor places in business and society.

variation: stroke placed to the right of the letter
interpretation: hastiness, impulsiveness, quickness, animation, etc. It is a confirmatory sign of these traits of character.

variation: stroke flies off from the letter to the right without touching it
interpretation: impatience. If the writing is angular, it betokens a person of quick temper. Such writers are intolerant of reproof or restraint, often nervous and irritable. Their remarks are stinging when annoyed or angered, for they are impulsive and quickly lose their tempers.

variation: stroke placed to the left of the letter
interpretation: procrastination. Writers have a natural tendency to put off work from day to day; they prefer waiting to doing; they are rarely on time; they often miss their engagements, and cannot be depended upon to fulfil their promises, however well meaning they may be.
obstinacy.
A hopeful person, inclined to be imaginative and fanciful, ambitious, has many irons in the fire, thinks more of the future than either of the past or the present.
Absent-mindedness, as well as a lively imagination. Such writers are often highly aspirational, and are fond of building air-castles.
Conceit or egotism.
Talkativeness.
Opinionatedness and obstinate convictions.

Sensitive pride. The writer is not necessarily sensitive in the usual meaning of that term (feelings are not necessarily easily wounded), but he is quick to resent any reflection upon his personal conduct or any adverse criticism of whatever to him is a matter of importance or pride.
Without special significance.

variation: noticeably evident and abundant throughout the writing
interpretation: A natural tendency to give, to give out, to throw off.

variation: noticeably evident and abundant, combined with chirography denoting kindliness and quick sympathies
interpretation: Generosity and self-sacrifice (a giving out of one's self for the sake of others).

variation: noticeably evident and abundant, combined with selfish traits
interpretation: Extravagance, a willingness to spend freely, a readiness to give abundantly (not necessarily for the sake of others, but for the satisfaction of one's self).
A good-natured disposition (candid and outspoken).
Secretiveness and a general lack of candor.
Determination and obstinacy.

variation: end with a hook and have a downward tendency
interpretation: Opinionated, perverse in his convictions, and insistent upon his own ideas. He has his special way of doing things and will tolerate no dictation.

Self-contained, not given to extravagance either in the spending of material goods or of their emotions. They may be generous and sympathetic, but never extravagantly so, nor to the sacrifice of their own welfare. They exercise discrimination in their generosity and kindness.
A love of approbation. The writer is appreciative of praise and likes to have his talents recognized.
More strongly evident love of praise and approbation, combined with more or less egotism and a high appreciation of one's own merits.
Conceit and egotism (come to the fore very prominently).

variation: flourish of inordinate proportions (as exemplified by Edgar Allan Poe's signature)
interpretation: Inordinate self-esteem.

Of negative significance; does not necessarily indicate opposite traits to those described (e.g., lack of self-esteem or egotism), as persons with much self-esteem or egotism may not indulge in any underscore.
activity, quickness
lacking sharpness, lacking haste (reverse of angular hand traits)
guided largely by impulses, governed by natural inclinations
inclined to question and deliberate
disinclination to be governed by impulse or authority
self-containment, reserve, mental culture, unobtrusive, compact, concentrated, held within bounds, orderly, concise, desire to avoid display, unostentatious, quiet
superficial, unreserved, restless, thoughtless, obtrusive, unrestrained, diffuse
aggressive, domineering, strong willed, masterful, exacting, sensuous
lacking the qualities of heavy writing (e.g., not aggressive, not domineering)

variation: spasmodic (heavy stroking of t, pressure on terminals)
interpretation: emphatic views, decided views, love of luxury, appreciation of physical pleasures, determined nature, insistent nature
egotism, love of display, instinctively conceited, lacking in niceties of refinement and culture, desire to draw attention to oneself through cheap or meretricious means
conventional, relatively little originality, relatively little initiative, does things according to rule and custom, opinions are reflections of others' ideas
simple, unaffected, modest, free from display or ostentation
neat, methodical, systematic
lack of method, lack of system, carelessness
quick, impulsive, hasty, vivacious, animated, intolerant of restraint or rules, talkative, original, careless of exact facts or statements, emotional
Saving disposition; close; secretive; introspective; narrow
Extravagance; superficiality; lack of reserve; wanting in concentration
Hopeful; optimistic; ambitious; generally buoyant nature
A disposition quite the reverse of the writer of ascending lines; depressed; borne down; lacks vigor or strength

Natural reasoner; mind works methodically and in continuity of thought; does not jump at conclusions; no gaps or breaks in reasoning or judgment; accepts nothing in the way of belief or conviction unless it presents a complete and unbroken line of facts and reasons
Lacks continuity of thought and action; jumps at conclusions; does not depend upon logical and definite reasoning for opinions
Open, disinterested nature; does not naturally find it easy to be reserved or close-mouthed
Fancy; extravagance; generosity; easiness; a departure from the conventional
Firmness; practicality; determination
Firmness; carefulness
Firmness; insistence; emphasis
Absence of the qualities shown by the firm and well made stroke
Wilful morbid firmness
Hope; aspiration

variation: Looped formation of the upper part of the letter
interpretation: Habit of allowing his thoughts or his emotions to be reflected back upon himself; sensitive; readily hurt in his pride

variation: Angular form combined with a decided forward slope
interpretation: Active, energetic, governed very largely by her feelings and affections.

variation: Closed a's and o's and carefully and distinctly formed letters
interpretation: Exhibits feelings and affections more frequently in action than in words; exercises a restraining influence; confines natural impulsiveness and quickness within more serious and practical bounds; self-contained; results in a high-strung temperament; inclined to be nervous.

variation: Careful and precise formation of the writing
interpretation: Painstaking disposition.

variation: Increasing size of letters toward the end of a word
interpretation: Extreme conscientiousness.
Perseverance.
energetic; fond of doing; always engaged upon some work or the planning of work
procrastinating
instinctive reticence, thoughtfulness, tact
intuitive
practical
artistic, gifted with a ready fancy, love for the arts
very affectionate nature (but never demonstrative)
generous, charitable, sympathetic, unselfish, self-sacrificing in her love and duties
thriftiness, sense of economy
tastes are plain, quiet, unextravagant

variation: generally rounded hand with a vertical tendency
interpretation: naturally inclined to be deliberate and indolent; to take life rather easily; working when necessary but rarely for the mere love of doing something

variation: angular formations (present here and there, not dominant)
interpretation: introduces an element of activity; contains a certain amount of energy and nervousness (offsetting natural deliberation)
restless disposition; moods are often uncertain and indefinite; wants to be doing and doesn't want to be doing
aesthetic nature; love of the beautiful; artistic sense

variation: good-sized loops of f and p
interpretation: imagination, fancy (confirms artistic sense)
practical nature, tendency toward the material
practical nature, tendency toward the material
practical nature, tendency toward the material
lacks method, lacks systematic plans
lacks method, lacks systematic plans; too easily diverted; mind is too restless; too susceptible to the influence of his surroundings; too impatient of logical and tedious reasoning
lacks faculty of concentration necessary for long-continued, methodical work
love of pleasure, easy-going, kindly, good-natured
lack of distinctive individuality; commonplace culture and attainments
methodical, tidy, careful
good judgment, business instincts
will-power
intuitive; hasty conclusions; indifference to rules or reasoning
uncommunicative on personal affairs
indifference to principles; readiness to waive a principle in favor of personal interest
lack of will-power or firmness; vacillation, changeableness, indecision, lack of self-reliance
quickness, impulsiveness; energy, activity, industry; affection, kindness of a practical sort
temperate tastes; natural refinement of feeling
carefulness, method, business instincts, neatness, attention to affairs
firmness, perseverance, and will-power
practical, logical, not readily convinced, argumentative, skeptical
conscientiousness; adherence to principles; high regard for duty
fancy, imagination, liveliness of mind, but all within reason and controlled by judgment and good sense

variation: d and t extend below line
interpretation: opinionated; insistent, determined
talkativeness; good qualities as a conversationalist; animated and emphatic talker
imagination, lively fancy, but tempered with the qualities of her practical nature
quick sympathies, generosity, readiness to give and to do for others
love of ease, appreciation of good living and luxuries, combined with activity, industry and a desire to do
inclined to be more or less deliberate, to weigh matters carefully; lack of impulsiveness or ready response to emotion
good mental faculties; cultured, inclined to be reserved; educated
assertive, determined, appreciation of material and physical pleasures
intuitive faculties, quickness of perception, ready arrival at conclusions; critical

variation: Capital M with tall third stroke
interpretation: nervousness, irritability, exacting and unreasonable nature in many respects
lack of candor; uncommunicative
sensitive; easily offended; morbid prejudices
physical agility; fondness for sports and outdoor recreations generally, and dancing
firmness, great will-power and determination
talkativeness
insistence and determination
activity, quickness and energy in a quiet way; sentiment and affection, kindliness and sympathy
culture, good mental qualities, education, reserve force
tendency to carelessness, lack of business methods
confirmation of traits shown in preceding feature
ambition, hopefulness, bright and cheery disposition
intuition and quick perceptions combined with reasoning and logical mind
self-appreciation
frankness, communicativeness; readiness to talk

variation: d with curved and hooked stroke
interpretation: literary tastes and abilities

variation: y, p, and g with long loops
interpretation: lively fancy, imagination, original ideas, unconventional opinions
physical quickness and activity, natural fondness for outdoor exercise, recreation, etc.
vivacity, impatience, quickness
talkativeness; bright and animated conversationalist and talker
pleasure-loving, fond of ease and luxury; appreciative of the beauties, the art, the comforts of life; sympathetic, affectionate, kind-hearted; impulsive

lack of business methods or systematic action; tendency to carelessness in matters of unimportance and trifles; rather circumscribed views and desires; little taste for general society; a saving, economical disposition
easily discouraged; subject to moods of depression in an otherwise bright and lively nature
conscientiousness; high sense of duty
ambitious for the success of others
uncommunicative; not generally confidential
critical disposition; introspective

variation: p and y with long loops
interpretation: lively fancy, original ideas, unconventional notions; fondness for poetry, music, etc.
love of ease and pleasure, indolence, and lack of energy, combined with quickness, desire for activity and a practical disposition
affectionate, sympathetic
emphatic, insistent
practical, systematic, methodical, prudent
clear judgment; sensible; not easily convinced against her reason
will-power and perseverance
intuitive, good reader of character; alert mind, ready perceptions
communicative, ready to talk
practical, not much fancy or imagination; determination
originality, practical nature, decisiveness
moderate love of sports, outdoor pleasure, etc.

variation: stroked heavily, weak in size, downward slope
interpretation: obstinacy, insistence, opinionatedness; difficult to convince; perverse
economical, selfish, calculating

easy-going, lack of energy, love of pleasure, ease, comfort and luxuries; appreciative of the good things of life; kindly, good-natured; quietly sympathetic, and loving; rarely demonstrative, and generally about the same from one day to another
a person of commonplace, ordinary attributes, with nothing distinctive one way or another
methodical, careful to follow directions and to do what is regarded as fashionable
economical, saving; prudent, naturally close-fisted and thrifty
mildly ambitious, hopeful, optimistic
slow thinker; wants a reason for whatever is done
lack of will-power or self-confidence; a personality without any decided views or convictions of its own
not readily generous; self-centred
changeableness, uncertainty of mood, impulsive and emotional
self-confidence, assertiveness; love of the physical and material pleasures of the world
impatience, rapidity of thought and action; originality; distinctive ability
intolerance of method or rules; carelessness in many ways
intolerance of method or rules; carelessness in many ways
highly intuitive; hasty decisions; little patience with argument or reason
self-appreciation, egotism
individuality, practical ideas
literary tastes and abilities
determination
will-power, insistence, determination
imagination, lively fancy; originality of ideas
industrious, active, energetic, practical, with love of ease, appreciation of comfort and luxury, and a tendency to take life easy
susceptibility to impulse
indifference to rules or engagements
more or less carelessness in certain directions; unstable decisions; intolerance of restraint or dictation
critical disposition, observant; a good reasoner; logical decisions in matters of importance
versatility; capable of doing many things well and perfectly
candor, frankness; outspoken opinions
pride of family or personal dignity
quickness; energy

variation: Stroked firmly and to the right of stem
interpretation: will-power, firmness, determination; impatience, activity

variation: Descends below line (t and other letters)
interpretation: insistence, obstinacy; hard to convince; opinionated
fanciful; unconventional ideas, fond of fads

variation: hard right upstrokes that replace a lower loop
interpretation: pushes forward into the future asserting himself physically. Often this person needs physically aggressive outlets such as competitive sports or even violence.

variation: slant that is midway between a vertical and a hard right-slant
interpretation: relates to both introverts and extroverts while keeping a level head even amidst the existence of emotional feelings such as empathy, sympathy, and even passion. Falls into the middle-of-the-road category on many issues.

variation: dots on the i and j being placed close to the stem
interpretation: more attention the writer pays to details. This trait contributes to a good memory. Notices everything.

variation: needlepoint strokes on the top of the n and m, also in the h or i
interpretation: lightning fast minds that are able to size up situations instantly. They are curious, impatient, intelligent and usually in a hurry.
ability to shut everything out of their mind and fully concentrate on one thing at a time. Often, on a first impression, these people seem reserved.

variation: large lower case letter, especially k, anywhere in the writing (high buckle k / "go to hell K")
interpretation: resists other's authority. Seen as resentment at being told what to do and as rebellion in young people.

variation: High ending strokes that go up in the air
interpretation: needs to be the center of attention and is always looking for ways to get noticed. This person needs lots of recognition.

variation: Downslanted humps toward the right on m's and n's
interpretation: ability to say things in a way that other people want to hear. He can also phrase touchy subjects in a nice, inoffensive way.

variation: Down slanted t-bar, to the right, with a blunt ending
interpretation: tends to be in control and likes it. He takes charge, making people follow directions without angering them.

variation: Down-slanted t-bar, to the right, with a sharp ending
interpretation: tends to fight for control. He takes charge, insisting people follow him. The sharp t-bar signifies sarcasm, whining, griping, and possible cruelness when he doesn't get his way. An unpleasant trait.

variation: obvious slant variation from left to right in the same sentence
interpretation: trouble making decisions because the head and heart are in conflict. He withdraws into the introverted personality when he feels insecure. Unpredictable mood swings.
inflated importance of himself (ego).
weak ego strength, thus affecting self-esteem.

variation: consistent leftward slant. The upstrokes and slant are a minimum of 90 degrees from the baseline.
interpretation: An introvert. This person keeps his own counsel, rarely expresses feelings, and makes logical, unemotional decisions.

heart-ruled, moody, impulsive person. The farther the writing slants to the right, the more this person is subject to emotional stimuli. This writer naturally relates to people’s emotions and has a constant need for affection. He can be very expressive with his emotions and relates to others easily.

variation: unusually long stroke that makes the cross bar of the t
interpretation: bubbles over with enthusiasm and excitement. Enthusiasm is a key ingredient to success in leadership, because it motivates others toward action.

variation: down-turned y or g that doesn't cross the baseline
interpretation: The closer it comes to the baseline, the closer the person will come to success, then turn away. Often a feeling of dejection occurs near success, thus this person gets very close to success, then fails.

variation: figure eight shape anywhere in the writing. Most commonly found in the lower loop of the g or in the capital letters of a signature.
interpretation: ability to follow and change thoughts smoothly. Often a good conversationalist, speaker, or writer.

variation: letter o that has no inner loops and is relatively wide
interpretation: will be honest and blunt when asked her opinion. If the o is open, then she will volunteer her frank opinion without being asked.

variation: long final strokes extending to the right at about a 45-degree angle and/or wide word spacing
interpretation: will be eager to share. This writer derives attention and personal reward from being generous to others.

variation: High crossed t-bar and large personal pronoun I
interpretation: confidence, ambition, the ability to plan ahead, high goals, high personal expectations, and an overall good self-image. This is the key to personal success and happiness.

variation: Large and wide lower loops in the y, g and j
interpretation: strong physical imagination, gullibility, and a tendency to exaggerate the tangible aspects of life. Has great ability to visualize and see things clearly.
philosophical imagination.

variation: short d or t-stem with a restricted or no loop
interpretation: has his own ideas about the way life is. He thinks independently of what others believe. His dress and choices in life tend to be made without regard to the norm.
youth is trying to stand out from the crowd.
fear of being ordinary and must call attention to herself by being different. Also, it can indicate artistic or creative expression.
The longer the slash, the more irritated the writer is at the time of the writing. Slash down is irritation at the present
